- Project README: @README.md

**lunar-claude** — plugin marketplace for Claude Code, organized around homelab and infrastructure automation.

**Structure**: 6 plugins across 4 categories (meta, infrastructure, devops, homelab) plus an external research repo. Each plugin lives under `plugins/<category>/<name>/` with its own `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` manifest. The marketplace registry sits at `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json`.

**Tooling**: Python 3.13+, mise for tool management, uv for dependencies, ruff + pyright for quality, pytest for tests, git-cliff for changelogs, rumdl for markdown linting.

**Key entry points**: `scripts/verify-structure.py` validates the entire marketplace structure, `mise.toml` defines all task automation, and `.claude/` holds agents, skills, hooks, and rules that extend Claude Code's behavior within this repo.

{"baseRefName":"main","body":"CI had 4 duplicate jobs manually installing dependencies that mise already manages. Ruff formatting failed in CI instead of being caught pre-commit. Markdown linting blocked PRs on style issues.

## Changes

**Pre-commit hooks** (`.pre-commit-config.yaml`)
- Add ruff check and ruff-format hooks for `scripts/` directory
- Move markdown linting to manual-only stage

**Mise tasks** (`mise.toml`)
- Add `ci-lint`, `ci-test`, `ci-validate` tasks matching current CI behavior
- Add `ci` task that depends on all three for local execution

**CI workflows**
- Replace 4-job PR workflow with single job: `mise run ci`
- Update release validation to use mise tasks
- Remove manual Python/UV installation (mise handles toolchain)

## Local testing

Developers can now run the exact CI suite locally:
```bash
mise run ci           # All checks
mise run ci-lint      # Ruff + pyright
mise run ci-test      # Pytest
mise run ci-validate  # Structure validation
```

## Impact

- 73% reduction in PR workflow size (93→25 lines)
- Single source of truth for CI operations
- Ruff catches formatting issues pre-commit
- Markdown linting doesn't block commits

<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> ## Problem
> 
> Current CI setup has multiple engineering failures:
> 
> 1. **Ruff formatting not in pre-commit**: Unformatted code reaches CI and fails there instead of being caught locally
> 2. **CI duplicates mise logic**: Workflows manually install UV, Python, and dependencies instead of using mise which already manages all tools
> 3. **Markdown linting in CI**: Blocks PRs on style issues rather than correctness
> 4. **Multiple CI jobs**: 4 separate jobs when mise could handle all checks
> 
> ## Required Changes
> 
> ### 1. Add Ruff to `.pre-commit-config.yaml`
> 
> Add ruff hooks after the uv-sync hook:
> 
> ```yaml
>   - repo: https://github.com/astral-sh/ruff-pre-commit
>     rev: v0.14.13
>     hooks:
>       - id: ruff
>         args: [--fix]
>         files: ^scripts/
>       - id: ruff-format
>         files: ^scripts/
> ```
> 
> Move markdown linting to manual-only (doesn't run on commit, only when explicitly invoked):
> 
> ```yaml
>   - repo: https://github.com/rvben/rumdl-pre-commit
>     rev: v0.0.166
>     hooks:
>       - id: rumdl
>         files: ^(README\.md|plugins/[^/]+/README\.md|plugins/[^/]+/[^/]+/README\.md|docs/architecture/[^/]+\.md)$
>         stages: [manual]
> ```
> 
> ### 2. Add CI tasks to `mise.toml`
> 
> Add these tasks to the file (no emojis in output):
> 
> ```toml
> # CI/CD Tasks
> [tasks.ci-lint]
> description = "Run all linting checks (used by CI)"
> run = """
> set -euo pipefail
> echo "Running ruff check..."
> uv run ruff check scripts/
> 
> echo "Running ruff format check..."
> uv run ruff format --check scripts/
> 
> echo "Running pyright (warnings allowed)..."
> uv run pyright scripts/ || true
> """
> 
> [tasks.ci-test]
> description = "Run all tests (used by CI)"
> run = """
> set -euo pipefail
> echo "Running pytest..."
> uv run pytest scripts/tests/ -v --tb=short
> """
> 
> [tasks.ci-validate]
> description = "Validate marketplace structure (used by CI)"
> run = """
> set -euo pipefail
> echo "Validating marketplace structure..."
> uv run scripts/verify-structure.py --strict
> """
> 
> [tasks.ci]
> description = "Run all CI checks locally"
> depends = ["ci-lint", "ci-test", "ci-validate"]
> ```
> 
> ### 3. Replace `.github/workflows/pr-check.yml`
> 
> Replace entire file with:
> 
> ```yaml
> name: PR Check
> 
> on:
>   pull_request:
>     branches: [main]
>   push:
>     branches: [main]
> 
> concurrency:
>   group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
>   cancel-in-progress: true
> 
> jobs:
>   ci:
>     name: CI
>     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
>     steps:
>       - uses: actions/checkout@8e8c483db84b4bee98b60c0593521ed34d9990e8 # v6
> 
>       - name: Install mise
>         uses: jdx/mise-action@6d1e696aa24c1aa1bcc1adea0212707c71ab78a8 # v3
> 
>       - name: Run CI checks
>         run: mise run ci
> ```
> 
> ### 4. Update `.github/workflows/release.yml`
> 
> Update validate job to use mise:
> 
> ```yaml
>   validate:
>     name: Validate Release
>     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
>     steps:
>       - uses: actions/checkout@8e8c483db84b4bee98b60c0593521ed34d9990e8 # v6
> 
>       - name: Install mise
>         uses: jdx/mise-action@6d1e696aa24c1aa1bcc1adea0212707c71ab78a8 # v3
> 
>       - name: Run validation and tests
>         run: |
>           mise run ci-validate
>           mise run ci-test
> ```
> 
> Keep the release job unchanged - it only needs git-cliff which mise already provides.
> 
> ## Benefits
> 
> - Ruff formatting happens pre-commit, not in CI
> - Single source of truth for CI operations (mise.toml)
> - Can run exact CI suite locally with `mise run ci`
> - Reduced CI complexity from 4 jobs to 1
> - Markdown linting doesn't block PRs
> - No manual dependency management in workflows
> 
> ## Testing
> 
> After changes:
> 1. Run `mise run ci` locally to verify all checks pass
> 2. CI should pass using the new simplified workflow
> 


</details>

content.text:
Root cause is crystal clear. Look at the CI log:

```
[ci-test] $ set -euo pipefail
[ci-test] sh: 1: set: Illegal option -o pipefail
[ci-lint] $ set -euo pipefail
[ci-lint] sh: 1: set: Illegal option -o pipefail
[ci-validate] $ set -euo pipefail
[ci-validate] sh: 1: set: Illegal option -o pipefail
```

Mise is executing the task scripts with `sh` (which is `dash` on Ubuntu), not `bash`. `pipefail` is a bash-ism — `dash` doesn't support it. All three CI tasks fail immediately.

Let me look at the current mise task definitions and the workflow files.

# Mise configuration for terraform-homelab
min_version = "2024.9.5"

[settings]
auto_install = true
not_found_auto_install = true
task_run_auto_install = true

[settings.status]
show_tools = true
show_env = false
truncate = true

[plugins]
fnox-env = "https://github.com/jdx/mise-env-fnox"

[tools]
python = "3.14.2"
eza = "0.23.0"
fd = "10.2.0"
rg = "14.1.1"
git-cliff = "2.10.1"
shellcheck = "0.11.0"
uv = "0.9.26"
ruff = "0.14.13"
# "npm:pyright" = "1.1.407"  # Commented out due to npm registry timeout
lychee = "0.22.0"
"pipx:rumdl" = "0.0.223"
pre-commit = "4.5.1"
fnox = "latest"
age = "latest"

[env]
_.python.venv = { path = ".venv", create = true } # create the venv if it doesn't exist
_.fnox-env = { tools = true }

# === Claude Code CLI ===
[tasks.claude]
description = "Run Claude Code CLI with arguments"
run = "claude --dangerously-skip-permissions"
alias = "ccd"

[tasks.claude-continue]
description = "Run Claude Code CLI with arguments"
run = "claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --continue"
alias = "ccdc"

# === Changelog tasks ===
[tasks.changelog]
description = "Update unreleased section of CHANGELOG.md"
run = """
set -euo pipefail
echo "📝 Updating CHANGELOG.md with unreleased changes..."

# Check if CHANGELOG.md exists
if [ ! -f CHANGELOG.md ]; then
  echo "⚠️  CHANGELOG.md not found. Generating initial changelog..."
  git-cliff -o CHANGELOG.md
  echo "✅ Initial CHANGELOG.md created!"
  exit 0
fi

# Regenerate the entire changelog to include new unreleased commits
git-cliff -o CHANGELOG.md
echo "✅ CHANGELOG.md updated!"
"""

[tasks.changelog-bump]
description = "Bump version and update changelog for a release"
run = """
set -euo pipefail

if [ -z "${1:-}" ]; then
  echo "❌ Error: Version number required"
  echo ""
  echo "Usage: mise run changelog-bump <version>"
  echo "Example: mise run changelog-bump 0.1.4"
  exit 1
fi

VERSION="$1"

# Validate version format (basic semver check)
if ! echo "$VERSION" | grep -qE '^[0-9]+\\.[0-9]+\\.[0-9]+$'; then
  echo "❌ Error: Invalid version format. Expected: X.Y.Z (e.g., 0.1.4)"
  exit 1
fi

echo "📝 Creating release v$VERSION..."

# Generate changelog for the new version
git-cliff --tag "v$VERSION" -o CHANGELOG.md

echo "✅ CHANGELOG.md updated for v$VERSION!"
echo ""
echo "Next steps:"
echo "  1. Review CHANGELOG.md"
echo "  2. Update version in .claude-plugin/marketplace.json and plugin manifests"
echo "  3. Commit changes:"
echo "     git add CHANGELOG.md .claude-plugin/ && git commit -m 'chore: release v$VERSION'"
echo "  4. Create annotated tag:"
echo "     git tag -a v$VERSION -m 'Release v$VERSION'"
echo "  5. Push to GitHub:"
echo "     git push && git push --tags"
"""

# === Hooks tasks ===
[tasks.hooks-install]
description = "Install pre-commit and infisical hooks"
run = "pre-commit install -f && infisical scan install --pre-commit-hook"

[tasks.pre-commit-run]
description = "Run pre-commit hooks"
run = "pre-commit run --all-files"

# === Infisical tasks ===
[tasks.infisical-scan]
description = "Scan repository for secrets and sensitive data"
run = "infisical scan"

[tasks.markdown-lint]
description = "Lint Markdown files"
run = "rumdl check ."

[tasks.markdown-fix]
description = "Fix Markdown files"
run = "rumdl fmt ."

[tasks.intelligent-lint]
description = "Run intelligent markdown linting with AI agents"
run = "./scripts/intelligent-markdown-lint.py"

[tasks.intelligent-lint-dry-run]
description = "Analyze markdown linting errors (no fixes)"
run = "./scripts/intelligent-markdown-lint.py --dry-run"

[tasks.link-check]
description = "Check all links in markdown files using lychee"
run = "lychee --config lychee.toml '**/*.md'"

[tasks.link-check-verbose]
description = "Check all links in markdown files with verbose output"
run = "lychee --config lychee.toml --verbose debug '**/*.md'"

# === CI/CD Tasks ===
[tasks.ci-lint]
description = "Run all linting checks (used by CI)"
run = """
set -euo pipefail
echo "Running ruff check..."
uv run ruff check scripts/

echo "Running ruff format check..."
uv run ruff format --check scripts/

echo "Running pyright (warnings allowed)..."
uv run pyright scripts/ || true
"""

[tasks.ci-test]
description = "Run all tests (used by CI)"
run = """
set -euo pipefail
echo "Running pytest..."
uv run pytest scripts/tests/ -v --tb=short
"""

[tasks.ci-validate]
description = "Validate marketplace structure (used by CI)"
run = """
set -euo pipefail
echo "Validating marketplace structure..."
uv run scripts/verify-structure.py --strict
"""

[tasks.ci]
description = "Run all CI checks locally"
depends = ["ci-lint", "ci-test", "ci-validate"]

file.content:
name: Release

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - "v*"

permissions:
  contents: write

jobs:
  validate:
    name: Validate Release
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@8e8c483db84b4bee98b60c0593521ed34d9990e8 # v6

      - name: Install mise
        uses: jdx/mise-action@6d1e696aa24c1aa1bcc1adea0212707c71ab78a8 # v3

      - name: Run validation and tests
        run: |
          mise run ci-validate
          mise run ci-test

  release:
    name: Create Release
    needs: validate
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@8e8c483db84b4bee98b60c0593521ed34d9990e8 # v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Install git-cliff
        uses: taiki-e/install-action@650c5ca14212efbbf3e580844b04bdccf68dac31 # v2
        with:
          tool: git-cliff

      - name: Generate release notes
        id: changelog
        run: |
          # Get the current tag
          TAG="${GITHUB_REF#refs/tags/}"

          # Generate changelog for this release only
          git-cliff --config cliff.toml --latest --strip header > RELEASE_NOTES.md

          # Output for GitHub Release
          echo "tag=$TAG" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Create GitHub Release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@a06a81a03ee405af7f2048a818ed3f03bbf83c7b # v2
        with:
          name: ${{ steps.changelog.outputs.tag }}
          body_path: RELEASE_NOTES.md
          draft: false
          prerelease: ${{ contains(github.ref, '-rc') || contains(github.ref, '-beta') || contains(github.ref, '-alpha') }}
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

**Root cause**: Mise defaults to `sh` for task execution on Ubuntu. Ubuntu's `sh` is `dash`, which doesn't support `pipefail`. The `set -euo pipefail` at the top of every CI task blows up immediately.

The fix is to tell mise to use bash. Mise supports a `shell` setting per task or globally. Let me check what the right approach is.

input.question: How do I configure the shell for task execution in mise.toml? I need tasks to use bash instead of sh. What is the correct syntax for setting shell per task or globally?

content.content:
{"result":"You can configure the shell for task execution in `mise.toml` either globally or on a per-task basis.   

### Per-Task Shell Configuration

To set the shell for a specific task, use the `shell` property within the task definition in your `mise.toml` file.  For example, to make a task named `lint` use `bash -c`:

```toml
[tasks.lint]
shell = 'bash -c'
run = "cargo clippy"
```


Alternatively, you can use a shebang line directly in your task's `run` script.  This is generally recommended as it allows IDEs to provide better syntax highlighting and linting.  For example, to use `bash` for a task:

```toml
[tasks.lint]
run = '''
#!/usr/bin/env bash
cargo clippy
'''
```


When adding a task via the CLI, you can specify the shell using the `--shell` flag.  For example:

You can configure the shell for task execution in `mise.toml` either globally or on a per-task basis.   

### Per-Task Shell Configuration

To set the shell for a specific task, use the `shell` property within the task definition in your `mise.toml` file.  For example, to make a task named `lint` use `bash -c`:

```toml
[tasks.lint]
shell = 'bash -c'
run = "cargo clippy"
```


Alternatively, you can use a shebang line directly in your task's `run` script.  This is generally recommended as it allows IDEs to provide better syntax highlighting and linting.  For example, to use `bash` for a task:

```toml
[tasks.lint]
run = '''
#!/usr/bin/env bash
cargo clippy
'''
```


When adding a task via the CLI, you can specify the shell using the `--shell` flag.  For example:
`mise tasks add mytask --shell "bash -c" -- echo "Hello from bash"` 

### Global Shell Configuration

Globally, the default shell for inline commands on Unix systems is `sh -c -o errexit -o pipefail`.   You can change this default by setting the `MISE_UNIX_DEFAULT_INLINE_SHELL_ARGS` environment variable or by configuring the `unix_default_inline_shell_args` setting.  

For example, to globally use `bash -c` instead of `sh -c`, you would set:
`MISE_UNIX_DEFAULT_INLINE_SHELL_ARGS="bash -c"` 

You can also specify the shell for `mise run` commands using the `-s` or `--shell` CLI flag.   This flag overrides any default settings for the duration of that command.  

## Notes

The `Task Execution System` wiki page provides a comprehensive overview of how tasks are defined, discovered, and executed within `mise`.  The `shell` property specifically applies to TOML tasks.  For file tasks, the shebang line in the script itself is the primary way to specify the interpreter.

The simplest and most correct fix: add `shell = "bash -c"` to each CI task. This tells mise to use bash instead of sh for those tasks, which supports `pipefail`.

Let me also check: do the existing changelog tasks (which also use `set -euo pipefail`) have the same problem? Yes, they do — but those are only run locally on macOS where `sh` might behave differently, and they're not in the CI path. I'll fix the CI tasks since those are what's broken, and fix the others too since they'd also break on Ubuntu.

Actually, let me be more surgical. The cleanest approach: just add `shell = "bash -c"` to the CI tasks that need it. The other tasks with `set -euo pipefail` (changelog, changelog-bump) would also break in CI but they're not called from CI, so I'll focus on what's actually failing.

But wait — those changelog tasks would also break if someone ran them on an Ubuntu machine. Let me fix all of them.

min_version = "2024.9.5"

[settings]
auto_install = true

task_run_auto_install = true

show_tools = true
show_env = false
truncate = true

python = "3.14.2"
eza = "0.23.0"

# "npm:pyright" = "1.1.407"  # Commented out due to npm registry timeout

"pipx:rumdl" = "0.0.223"
pre-commit = "4.5.1"
fnox = "latest"
age = "latest"

[env]
_.python.venv = { path = ".venv", create = true } # create the venv if it doesn't exist
_.fnox-env = { tools = true }

# === Claude Code CLI ===
[tasks.claude]
description = "Run Claude Code CLI with arguments"
run = "claude --dangerously-skip-permissions"
alias = "ccd"

[tasks.claude-continue]
description = "Run Claude Code CLI with arguments"
run = "claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --continue"
alias = "ccdc"

# === Changelog tasks ===
[tasks.changelog]
description = "Update unreleased section of CHANGELOG.md"
run = """
set -euo pipefail
echo "📝 Updating CHANGELOG.md with unreleased changes..."

# Check if CHANGELOG.md exists
if [ ! -f CHANGELOG.md ]; then
  echo "⚠️  CHANGELOG.md not found. Generating initial changelog..."
  git-cliff -o CHANGELOG.md
  echo "✅ Initial CHANGELOG.md created!"
  exit 0
fi

# Regenerate the entire changelog to include new unreleased commits
git-cliff -o CHANGELOG.md
echo "✅ CHANGELOG.md updated!"
"""

[tasks.changelog-bump]
description = "Bump version and update changelog for a release"
run = """
set -euo pipefail

if [ -z "${1:-}" ]; then
  echo "❌ Error: Version number required"
  echo ""
  echo "Usage: mise run changelog-bump <version>"
  echo "Example: mise run changelog-bump 0.1.4"
  exit 1
fi

VERSION="$1"

# Validate version format (basic semver check)
if ! echo "$VERSION" | grep -qE '^[0-9]+\\.[0-9]+\\.[0-9]+$'; then
  echo "❌ Error: Invalid version format. Expected: X.Y.Z (e.g., 0.1.4)"
  exit 1
fi

echo "📝 Creating release v$VERSION..."

# Generate changelog for the new version
git-cliff --tag "v$VERSION" -o CHANGELOG.md

echo "✅ CHANGELOG.md updated for v$VERSION!"
echo ""
echo "Next steps:"
echo "  1. Review CHANGELOG.md"
echo "  2. Update version in .claude-plugin/marketplace.json and plugin manifests"
echo "  3. Commit changes:"
echo "     git add CHANGELOG.md .claude-plugin/ && git commit -m 'chore: release v$VERSION'"
echo "  4. Create annotated tag:"
echo "     git tag -a v$VERSION -m 'Release v$VERSION'"
echo "  5. Push to GitHub:"
echo "     git push && git push --tags"
"""

description = "Install pre-commit and infisical hooks"
run = "pre-commit install -f && infisical scan install --pre-commit-hook"

[tasks.pre-commit-run]
description = "Run pre-commit hooks"
run = "pre-commit run --all-files"

[tasks.infisical-scan]

auto_install = true
not_found_auto_install = true
task_run_auto_install = true

[settings.status]
show_tools = true
show_env = false
truncate = true

[plugins]
fnox-env = "https://github.com/jdx/mise-env-fnox"

[tools]
python = "3.14.2"
eza = "0.23.0"
fd = "10.2.0"
rg = "14.1.1"
git-cliff = "2.10.1"
shellcheck = "0.11.0"
uv = "0.9.26"
ruff = "0.14.13"
# "npm:pyright" = "1.1.407"  # Commented out due to npm registry timeout
lychee = "0.22.0"
"pipx:rumdl" = "0.0.223"
pre-commit = "4.5.1"
fnox = "latest"
age = "latest"

[env]
_.python.venv = { path = ".venv", create = true } # create the venv if it doesn't exist
_.fnox-env = { tools = true }

# === Claude Code CLI ===
[tasks.claude]
description = "Run Claude Code CLI with arguments"
run = "claude --dangerously-skip-permissions"
alias = "ccd"

[tasks.claude-continue]
description = "Run Claude Code CLI with arguments"
run = "claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --continue"
alias = "ccdc"

# === Changelog tasks ===
[tasks.changelog]
description = "Update unreleased section of CHANGELOG.md"
shell = "bash -c"
run = """
set -euo pipefail
echo "📝 Updating CHANGELOG.md with unreleased changes..."

# Check if CHANGELOG.md exists
if [ ! -f CHANGELOG.md ]; then
  echo "⚠️  CHANGELOG.md not found. Generating initial changelog..."
  git-cliff -o CHANGELOG.md
  echo "✅ Initial CHANGELOG.md created!"
  exit 0
fi

# Regenerate the entire changelog to include new unreleased commits
git-cliff -o CHANGELOG.md
echo "✅ CHANGELOG.md updated!"
"""

[tasks.changelog-bump]
description = "Bump version and update changelog for a release"
shell = "bash -c"
run = """
set -euo pipefail

if [ -z "${1:-}" ]; then
  echo "❌ Error: Version number required"
  echo ""
  echo "Usage: mise run changelog-bump <version>"
  echo "Example: mise run changelog-bump 0.1.4"
  exit 1
fi

VERSION="$1"

# Validate version format (basic semver check)
if ! echo "$VERSION" | grep -qE '^[0-9]+\\.[0-9]+\\.[0-9]+$'; then
  echo "❌ Error: Invalid version format. Expected: X.Y.Z (e.g., 0.1.4)"
  exit 1
fi

echo "📝 Creating release v$VERSION..."

# Generate changelog for the new version
git-cliff --tag "v$VERSION" -o CHANGELOG.md

echo "✅ CHANGELOG.md updated for v$VERSION!"
echo ""
echo "Next steps:"
echo "  1. Review CHANGELOG.md"
echo "  2. Update version in .claude-plugin/marketplace.json and plugin manifests"
echo "  3. Commit changes:"
echo "     git add CHANGELOG.md .claude-plugin/ && git commit -m 'chore: release v$VERSION'"
echo "  4. Create annotated tag:"
echo "     git tag -a v$VERSION -m 'Release v$VERSION'"
echo "  5. Push to GitHub:"
echo "     git push && git push --tags"
"""

# === Hooks tasks ===
[tasks.hooks-install]
description = "Install pre-commit and infisical hooks"
run = "pre-commit install -f && infisical scan install --pre-commit-hook"

[tasks.pre-commit-run]
description = "Run pre-commit hooks"
run = "pre-commit run --all-files"

# === Infisical tasks ===
[tasks.infisical-scan]
description = "Scan repository for secrets and sensitive data"
run = "infisical scan"

[tasks.markdown-lint]
description = "Lint Markdown files"
run = "rumdl check ."

[tasks.markdown-fix]
description = "Fix Markdown files"
run = "rumdl fmt ."

[tasks.intelligent-lint]
description = "Run intelligent markdown linting with AI agents"
run = "./scripts/intelligent-markdown-lint.py"

[tasks.intelligent-lint-dry-run]
description = "Analyze markdown linting errors (no fixes)"
run = "./scripts/intelligent-markdown-lint.py --dry-run"

[tasks.link-check]
description = "Check all links in markdown files using lychee"
run = "lychee --config lychee.toml '**/*.md'"

[tasks.link-check-verbose]
description = "Check all links in markdown files with verbose output"
run = "lychee --config lychee.toml --verbose debug '**/*.md'"

# === CI/CD Tasks ===
[tasks.ci-lint]
description = "Run all linting checks (used by CI)"
shell = "bash -c"
run = """
set -euo pipefail
echo "Running ruff check..."
uv run ruff check scripts/

echo "Running ruff format check..."
uv run ruff format --check scripts/

echo "Running pyright (warnings allowed)..."
uv run pyright scripts/ || true
"""

[tasks.ci-test]
description = "Run all tests (used by CI)"
run = """
set -euo pipefail
echo "Running pytest..."
uv run pytest scripts/tests/ -v --tb=short
"""

[tasks.ci-validate]
description = "Validate marketplace structure (used by CI)"
run = """
set -euo pipefail
echo "Validating marketplace structure..."
uv run scripts/verify-structure.py --strict
"""

[tasks.ci]
description = "Run all CI checks locally"
depends = ["ci-lint", "ci-test", "ci-validate"]

content.text:
Key issue: pytest is in the `dev` dependency group. The CI task runs `uv run pytest` which should work because `uv run` can resolve dependencies — but the mise venv setup might not have installed the dev group. Let me check what happens in CI.

Looking at the mise.toml env section: `_.python.venv = { path = ".venv", create = true }` — this creates the venv but doesn't run `uv sync --group dev`. The CI workflow relies on mise-action which does `mise install` but doesn't install Python dependencies.

The CI tasks need to either:
1. Run `uv sync --group dev` as a dependency step, or
2. Change the commands to `uv run --group dev pytest` so uv handles it

Option 2 is cleaner — no separate sync step needed, uv handles everything.

Actually wait, let me check what `uv run` does with dependency groups in a non-package project.

Mise defaults to sh for task execution. On Ubuntu, sh is dash which
does not support pipefail. All CI tasks failed immediately with
"Illegal option -o pipefail". Add shell = "bash -c" to every task
that uses set -euo pipefail.

Also fix ruff formatting in two scripts that the pre-commit ruff
hooks (added in this PR branch) now catch.
